title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
On a terrace, Betty decides to end her relationship with Basil. She removes a symbolic ring given to her by Bazelton and asks Mrs. Chester to return it to him, requesting that Mrs. Chester tell him she is fine and wish him luck. When Basil returns at sunset, he and Mrs. Chester argue; Basil attributes their recent difficulties to jealousy, while Mrs. Chester fears he intends to marry a girl she does not approve of.

Seeking help, Mrs. Chester consults Mr. Parker Pyne. He agrees to intervene without Basil's knowledge. By midnight, Mr. Parker Pyne informs Mrs. Chester that he has influenced Miss Deloris Lamorna to leave Bollensa and the island shortly. He explains that he achieved this through his influence rather than money. He predicts that while Basil will be disappointed by her sudden departure, Betty will eventually forgive him.

On a steamship departing Palma, Mr. Parker Pyne reveals to Deloris Lamorna that she is actually the actress Madeline Salieri. Basil Chester approaches Mr. Parker Pyne to express his gratitude for the plan, which has resulted in his mother and Betty becoming friends. Basil asks about a Miss Salazar, but Mr. Parker Pyne informs him that she is leaving for America to assist with his business.

In a different setting, Joan Ashby and Harry Dahlhausen argue about whether a butler, Digby, rang a gong once or twice. Harry notes that his uncle has been acting strangely. Following the argument, a loud sound resembling a gunshot occurs. A dark-skinned man named Keane asks about the noise, which the butler claims was a car backfiring. However, Joan and Keane disagree on the direction of the sound, with Harry suggesting it came from the north.
